title: Steal the Seventh Tide
tagline: A disgraced navigator recruits a crew to steal a lost tide from an undersea palace.

cast.name: Selke Marr
cast.role: Disgraced navigator and architect of the seventh-tide heist
cast.visualDescription: Commanding young woman with deep brown skin, black curls braided into a high navigator's knot with small gold rings, long midnight-teal pressure coat over close black diving layers, luminous compass chains and a rolled star-current chart; sharp forward triangular silhouette with one hand always braced on the map.

cast.name: Nima Coral
cast.role: Former Pearl Court architect who can reshape living coral
cast.visualDescription: Tall dark-skinned architect with close-cropped silver curls, asymmetric lavender coral ear-crown, sleeveless blue-violet pressure tunic and articulated pearl forearm guards; carries crescent coral calipers and a spool of glowing plan-thread, creating a clean crescent-and-line silhouette.

cast.name: Iri Sable
cast.role: Pressure singer whose resonance opens the Pearl Court's living locks
cast.visualDescription: Lithe warm-brown pressure singer with long indigo-black locs tied low through silver resonance rings, a pearl throat collar, cropped coral-orange sound mantle over an ink-blue diving suit, and twin shell tuning forks; luminous circular pressure notes create a tall wave-and-ring silhouette without obscuring the face.

cast.name: Jory Venn
cast.role: Gifted dryland thief managing a severe fear of deep water
cast.visualDescription: Compact young man with medium-brown skin, short unruly black hair, slate-gray burglar layers under a ribbed pressure harness, soft-soled boots and too many lock tools; carries a small brass reserve-air bulb close to his chest, giving his guarded silhouette one bright circular anchor.

storefront.tileSubtitle: Rob a palace and return the ocean's highest tide
storefront.keyArtPrompt: A cinematic ocean-fantasy heist illustration of disgraced navigator Selke Marr leading a four-person crew through a pressure-glass tunnel toward the luminous Pearl Court beneath moonlit waves; turquoise living coral architecture, midnight-blue depths, pearl-white palace, bioluminescent masquerade masks, a vast imprisoned current coiling like a living ribbon and the subtle silhouette of a leviathan below, clever adventurous tone, no text, no logos, portrait composition with strong ensemble silhouettes.

prologueFrames.title: An Ocean One Tide Short
paragraphs: The world remembers seven tides, but only six still answer the moon. Since the Pearl Court captured the highest, harbors have filled with silt, reefs have starved, and inland rulers buy every cup through royal licenses.
Selke Marr once charted routes for that system and lost her navigator's seal when she challenged it. Now the dead harbor outside her window gives disgrace a more urgent name: debt.
prologueFrames.semanticLabel: Selke studies six tide marks above a silted harbor while a seventh empty line gleams under moonlight.

prologueFrames.title: What the Court Keeps
paragraphs: A stolen palace chart confirms Selke's suspicion: the seventh tide was never a royal treasure. It is a living current, folded beneath the undersea palace and forced to feed a continent of licensed canals.
A once-in-a-decade eclipse will open one route to its prison. Selke has the map and a narrow window, but no thief survives the Pearl Court alone.
prologueFrames.semanticLabel: A palace chart reveals a luminous living current coiled beneath the Pearl Court.

prologueFrames.title: A Crew for the Deep
paragraphs: Selke calls on Nima Coral, the architect who shaped the palace; Iri Sable, a singer who opens pressure locks; and Jory Venn, a dryland thief who can steal anything except a calm breath underwater.
Each has a reason to hate the Court and a reason not to trust Selke. Above them, the moon begins its slow passage toward shadow.
prologueFrames.semanticLabel: Selke, Nima, Iri, and Jory gather around a glowing heist map as the eclipse begins above the sea.

chapters.title: A Crew for a Living Current
chapters.caption: Turn four private motives into one workable plan.
paragraphs: Selke tests the crew against a model of the palace: Nima reshapes coral passages, Iri holds pressure doors on a note, and Jory crosses the mechanism without touching water. Their separate solutions work only after Selke charts them as one continuous route.

Royal license collectors surround the hideout behind Brinehorn Bailiff, a salt-armored harbor guardian that tracks unpaid water through its branching crown. The crew combines all four specialties to send it charging after a decoy current, escaping with their plan and their first earned trust.
chapters.semanticLabel: The four thieves face a salt-armored antlered harbor guardian beside a glowing model of the Pearl Court.

encounters.name: Tariff Barnacle
encounters.description: A waist-high cluster of salt-white barnacles plated with stamped license coins, opening concentric collector mouths around any droplet that crosses its chained harbor-stone base.

encounters.name: Permit Pistol-Shrimp
encounters.description: A bright brass shrimp with one enormous pearl-seal claw, snapping compressed-water stamps onto unlicensed currents while tiny filing drawers fan along its tail.

chapters.title: The Eclipse Route
chapters.caption: Steal the sequence that exists for one moonless hour.
paragraphs: The entry route is stored in a garden that grows a new navigation sequence with every lunar phase. Selke and Nima align the eclipse shadows while Jory lifts the sequence from a pearl that closes whenever it senses fear.

The garden knots itself into The Route-Coral Warden, sealing every passage Nima once designed. She breaks her old pattern instead of repeating it, and a route no palace architect anticipated blooms through the reef.
chapters.semanticLabel: The crew navigates a moonlit coral maze as its living branches rise into a towering warden.

encounters.name: Blueprint Polyp
encounters.description: A walking turquoise coral polyp with rolled palace plans for tentacles, growing walls exactly where Nima's old measurements say an intruder should turn.

encounters.name: Moonmaze Nudibranch
encounters.description: A long midnight nudibranch whose luminous back fronds show successive lunar phases, leaving a temporary silver route that curls shut behind its tail.

chapters.title: Masks Beneath the Waves
chapters.caption: Steal a shifting floor plan in a room full of watchers.
paragraphs: Disguised in bioluminescent masks, the crew enters the Pearl Court's eclipse masquerade. Selke navigates Regent Nacre's layered courtesies while Jory copies the palace plan from dances that make the ballroom floor rearrange.

Nacre releases The Masque Manta, a jeweled watcher that reads false identities in the water around each guest. Iri changes the orchestra's pressure-song, giving the crew one honest heartbeat in which every mask appears equally true.
chapters.semanticLabel: Masked dancers swirl through a flooded ballroom beneath a vast jeweled manta with luminous wings.

encounters.name: Gossip Fanfish
encounters.description: A small jewel-blue fish with enormous folding ballroom-fan fins, each rib displaying a guest's mask while its whisper bubbles swim toward the royal watchers.

encounters.name: Pearlmask Crown
encounters.description: Eight hand-sized porcelain mask fish orbiting an empty center like a crown, turning in perfect unison so one staring face always follows every dancer.

chapters.title: The Drowned Galleries
chapters.caption: Cross the defenses Nima built and Jory fears.
paragraphs: The stolen plan leads through galleries the Court floods at random, forcing Nima to reshape her own defenses while Jory moves from one shrinking pocket of air to the next. Selke refuses to rush him, even as the eclipse route closes behind them.

The lowest chamber gathers their fear into The Blackwater Curator, an abyssal figure that offers each thief a dry exit in exchange for abandoning the others. Jory names the trick first and crosses the final flooded span to open a path for the whole crew.
chapters.semanticLabel: Jory crosses a flooded gallery toward an abyssal curator while his crewmates wait in a shrinking air pocket.

encounters.name: Airpocket Sea Cucumber
encounters.description: A broad black sea cucumber with a row of shrinking air blisters along its back, firing narrow water jets that push swimmers toward convincing dry doorways.

encounters.name: Exit-Sign Cuttle
encounters.description: A translucent cuttlefish whose rectangular mantle projects a warm dry EXIT in familiar handwriting, while its pale arms quietly point away from the crew.

chapters.title: The Living Lock
chapters.caption: Open a vault that judges what every thief intends.
paragraphs: The tide vault reads hidden intent through coral, resonance, touch, and current. Nima, Iri, Jory, and Selke must each expose a private motive, then arrange those truths into one promise to return the tide rather than possess it.

Their contradictions ignite The Vent-Crowned Judge, a furnace-bright guardian fed by every lie they still tell themselves. Selke admits that revenge brought her this far but cannot guide what comes next, cooling the lock enough for the crew's shared purpose to open it.
chapters.semanticLabel: The crew stands before a living coral lock as a crown of volcanic vents burns above their reflected motives.

encounters.name: Truthglass Limpet
encounters.description: A low transparent limpet with four contradictory motives visible as colored figures beneath its shell, clamping to the vault whenever one tries to hide.

encounters.name: Perjury Chimneyworm
encounters.description: A tall coral tube worm crowned with orange hydrothermal chimneys, each feathery plume darkening and venting a boiling speech bubble when it hears a lie.

chapters.title: The Regent's Warning
chapters.caption: Redesign the theft around a danger the tyrant did not invent.
paragraphs: Regent Nacre meets the crew inside the vault and reveals the pressure readings beneath the palace: the imprisoned tide also weighs down a sleeping leviathan. Releasing it at once would restore the coasts and wake a creature large enough to break them.

Nacre seals the chamber with The Pearl Orrery, a mechanical model that permits only two futures—endless royal control or immediate ruin. Selke forces its interlocked moons into a third alignment, proving the tide can be moved gradually if the crew stays inside to guide it.
chapters.semanticLabel: Selke and Regent Nacre face a pearl-and-brass orrery above the shadow of a sleeping leviathan.

encounters.name: Moongear Nautilus
encounters.description: A pearl-and-brass nautilus whose spiral shell is a nested lunar mechanism, clicking the water between two rigid channels whenever its black and white eyes align.

encounters.name: Binary Starfish
encounters.description: A large five-armed starfish tiled half moon-white and half trench-black, flipping every arm at once so projected futures alternate between only captivity and ruin.

chapters.title: The Seventh Tide Runs Free
chapters.caption: Unfold a living current without letting the prison tear it apart.
paragraphs: The crew releases one fold of the seventh tide at a time, sending its first surge toward starving reefs and silted harbors. Iri sings the pressure sequence while Selke steers a current that has spent generations learning to fear every wall.

The broken prison rises as The Aftertide Gaoler, a spectral copy of every channel used to force the tide into obedience. Nima dissolves her last controlling architecture, and the living current runs free even as something immense opens an eye below.
chapters.semanticLabel: A luminous living tide breaks through ghostly prison channels while a leviathan's eye opens in the abyss.

encounters.name: Chainwake Comb-Jelly
encounters.description: A ribbed spherical comb jelly with ghostly prison bars instead of cilia, trailing broken channel chains that keep reforming around the living current.

encounters.name: Channelshade Lobster
encounters.description: A long blue-black lobster with canal-wall claws and a segmented lock-gate tail, crouching sideways across every opening the tide remembers as forbidden.

chapters.title: The Moving Ritual
chapters.caption: Restore the high tide while leading an ancient danger home.
paragraphs: With the palace breaking around them, the crew distributes the seventh tide through reefs, ports, and inland channels in a sequence that becomes their escape route. Each restored waterway draws the awakened leviathan away from populated coasts and toward an ancient trench.

Vask the Moonblind rises beneath the final current, following pressure older than any chart while the trench gates fail to align. Selke trusts her crew to finish the route without her command, and together they turn the restored tide into a moving ritual that guides Vask safely into the deep.
chapters.semanticLabel: The crew rides a luminous tide above the moonblind leviathan as restored waterways converge on a deep ocean trench.

encounters.name: Trenchbell Coelacanth
encounters.description: A massive slate coelacanth with a bronze pressure bell suspended inside its rib cage, each slow toll sending an ancient migration command through the trench.

encounters.name: Blindmoon Siphonophore
encounters.description: A ribbon-long colony of translucent lunar bells descending into darkness, with no eyes and one pearl lure moving through its bodies like a remembered moon.

finaleFrames.title: The High Water Returns
paragraphs: The seventh tide reaches harbors that had forgotten the sound of deep water. Silt lifts, reef polyps open, and royal license gates stand useless as the living current chooses its own wide course.
Far below, Vask settles into the restored trench, held not by a palace prison but by the pressure and song of a healed ocean.
finaleFrames.semanticLabel: A bright high tide restores a harbor and reef while the leviathan rests peacefully in a distant trench.

finaleFrames.title: No One Owns the Route
paragraphs: Regent Nacre loses the monopoly she defended, though her warning becomes part of the new charts rather than a secret buried with her court. Ports and inland towns agree to tend the channels together and publish every change.
Selke does not ask for her old seal back. With Nima, Iri, and a proudly seasick Jory beside her, she draws a new navigator's mark: an open route that no crown can close.
finaleFrames.semanticLabel: Selke's crew draws an open route on a shared harbor chart as moonlit water rises behind them.
